title: Thinking the Unthinkable with Ankit Panda Archives
gist: An archive index of the 'Thinking the Unthinkable with Ankit Panda' podcast series, whose episode topics map the 2025–2026 nuclear-strategy agenda: Iran's threshold, China's expansion, allied deterrence, and arms control.

insights.claim: The 'Thinking the Unthinkable' series centers late-2025 to mid-2026 nuclear discourse on Iran's nuclear threshold, China's nuclear expansion, North Korea's deterrence, and the future of arms control after New START.
insights.speaker: War on the Rocks
insights.so_what: Maps which nuclear-proliferation questions experts treat as most pressing for the period.
insights.locator: Tag: Thinking the Unthinkable with Ankit Panda

insights.claim: A cluster of episodes on Britain's, France's, Japan's, South Korea's, and Europe's nuclear options signals rising interest in extended deterrence and independent allied nuclear capabilities.
insights.speaker: War on the Rocks
insights.so_what: Growing coverage of allied nuclear hedging is an early indicator of proliferation pressure among US allies.
insights.locator: Europe’s Nuclear Options in a Changing World

insights.claim: The May 22, 2026 episode 'Why the Experts Missed China's Nuclear Expansion' flags an analytic-consensus failure to anticipate the pace of China's arsenal growth.
insights.speaker: War on the Rocks
insights.so_what: A reminder that prevailing estimates have understated China's nuclear buildup.
insights.locator: Why the Experts Missed China’s Nuclear Expansion
